01 Executive Summary

01
Executive Summary
Where Green APU stands today and what the opportunity looks like

Green APU sits in an enviable position: a niche product category (diesel APUs for semi trucks), a 15-year brand history, and genuine differentiation in parts availability and serviceability. The Ahrefs data tells a credible story — 1,380 organic keywords, $176,430 in traffic value, and 76 keywords ranking in positions 1–3 — for a product that costs $8,000–$12,000 installed. This is not a broken site. It is a performing one that is leaving significant revenue on the table.

The core problem is a dual gap: the site was built and optimized for the SEO era of 2018–2022. It ranks for transactional and brand terms, but it does not own the educational and comparative query space that now drives the top of the purchase funnel. More critically, it has essentially zero Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) infrastructure — no schema markup for products or FAQs, no structured comparison content, and no entity clarity signals that would cause ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Google AI Overviews to cite Green APU when truckers ask AI systems for APU recommendations.

The Core Opportunity

Green APU already has brand authority in the trucking community. The Riviera Finance review, forum citations on TruckersReport, and long-standing domain age give it third-party credibility that competitors are still building. The task is not to build authority from zero — it is to translate existing authority into AI-era citation infrastructure and to expand the content footprint to capture buyers earlier in their decision journey.

1,380
Organic Keywords Ranking
76
Keywords in Positions 1–3
$176K
Monthly Traffic Value
$0
Paid Search Investment

The $176K/month in estimated traffic value with zero paid spend is a strong signal that organic SEO is working. The challenge is that paid search = 0 also means there is no paid safety net, and organic-only strategies are increasingly exposed to AI search disruption. The recommendations in this audit are designed to both protect existing organic rankings and build the AEO infrastructure that will determine brand visibility over the next three to five years.

02
SEO Foundation Audit
Page-by-page assessment of on-site SEO health

What's Working

Brand + Transactional Rankings
Pass

Green APU ranks well for its own brand terms and for high-commercial-intent phrases like "truck APU," "semi truck APU," and "auxiliary power unit for trucks." These are the right keywords to own and conversion intent is high at this stage.

Domain Age & Authority
Pass

Founded 2011, active web presence since early 2010s. Domain age is a trust signal that newer competitors like HP2000 APU and RoyPow Electric APU cannot replicate quickly. This should be leveraged more explicitly in content and About page positioning.

Core Page Coverage
Pass

The site covers the essential pages: product page, installation, financing, service/support, blog, parts store, and location. The navigation is logical and the URL structure is clean (no parameter bloat observed). A solid structural foundation exists.

Third-Party Citations
Pass

The Riviera Finance APU comparison review, TruckersReport forum mentions, and Fontaine Modification distributor partnership provide meaningful external link equity. These third-party endorsements are the raw material for AEO entity establishment.

What Needs Work

Blog Content Is Thin & Dated

The blog has posts but most are 400–700 word articles written in 2019–2024 that function as SEO placeholders rather than authoritative resources. Titles like "What is an APU for a Truck?" rank for low-competition informational terms but do not build the topical depth needed to compete for high-value buyer-journey keywords or to be cited by AI systems. Articles use shortcodes in the visible source ([vc_row], [vc_column_text]) — a sign the page builder is leaking content markup into rendered pages, which can confuse crawlers.

Warning
No FAQ / Q&A Schema Markup

Zero structured FAQ markup was found across the site. This is the single highest-leverage technical fix available. FAQ schema enables Google AI Overviews to cite specific answers from Green APU pages, and it provides clean structured data for Perplexity and ChatGPT to parse. In the APU category, "how long does an APU pay for itself," "what is the best APU for a semi truck," and "how much fuel does an APU use" are active AI search queries with no dominant answer source. This is a gap that can be captured quickly.

Critical
No Product Schema / Structured Data

The product page does not include Product schema with price range, availability, manufacturer, or review aggregate data. This means Green APU is invisible in rich results and Google Shopping snippets, and does not feed the structured entity data that AI search engines use to build product knowledge graphs. Competitor sites that add this first will have a compounding advantage.

Critical
Missing Comparison & Versus Pages

There is no content directly addressing "Green APU vs. Thermo King TriPac," "Green APU vs. Carrier ComfortPro," or "diesel APU vs. electric APU." These comparison queries are among the most common mid-funnel searches in this category and are the exact format that AI answer engines preferentially cite. The buyer asking an AI "which APU should I buy?" needs comparative content to trigger a Green APU citation.

Critical
Savings Calculator Exists — But Isn't Crawlable

Green APU has a savings calculator, which is a genuine conversion asset. However, the calculator content is JavaScript-rendered and not backed by a static, crawlable companion page that explains the math in plain text. AI systems and search bots cannot parse dynamic JS calculators. A companion page with the fuel savings math written out as structured prose would allow this content to rank and be cited.

Warning
Visual Page Builder Markup Leakage

Several blog posts contain visible shortcode strings ([vc_column_text], [vc_row]) in the rendered page source. This indicates a WordPress Visual Composer / WPBakery configuration issue. These strings do not render in the browser but they do appear in source markup and can create parsing confusion for search engine crawlers. This should be audited and cleaned in all affected posts.

Warning
Fleet Manager Segment Is Underserved

The current site speaks primarily to owner-operators and individual truck drivers. Fleet-focused content (ROI calculators, total cost of ownership for 10+ truck fleets, compliance with state idling laws by fleet) is completely absent. Fleet managers are high-value buyers with multiple-unit purchase potential and are increasingly using AI research tools for procurement decisions. This is a major untapped segment.

Warning
LocalBusiness Schema Missing

Green APU has a physical installation facility in Lemont, IL and a growing distributor network. The site has no LocalBusiness schema, which means it does not appear in "APU installation near me" or "APU installer Illinois" local pack results. Given the installation requirement of APUs, local search intent is substantial and currently uncaptured.

Critical
03
Keyword Opportunity Analysis
Priority targets by intent, volume, and competitive feasibility

The following keyword clusters represent the highest-opportunity gaps between what Green APU currently ranks for and what buyers are actively searching. These are organized by funnel stage.

High-Intent Commercial Keywords (Underranked or Missing)

Keyword Est. Monthly Volume Intent Current Position Priority
best APU for semi trucks 2025 2,400–4,000 Commercial Not ranking High
diesel APU vs electric APU 800–1,500 Comparison Not ranking High
how much does an APU save on fuel 600–1,200 Informational Partial (savings page) High
APU for semi truck cost installed 900–1,800 Transactional Not ranking High
Green APU vs Thermo King 300–600 Comparison Not ranking High
Kubota diesel APU truck 400–800 Product Unknown Medium
truck APU anti-idling law compliance 200–500 Informational Not ranking Medium
fleet APU ROI calculator 150–350 Commercial Not ranking Medium
APU installation near me 500–1,000 Local Not ranking Medium
how does an APU work on a semi truck 300–700 Informational Blog post (weak) Medium
APU semi truck weight exemption by state 200–450 Informational Not ranking Low–Medium
APU for truck driver comfort winter 200–400 Informational Partial (existing blog) Low–Medium
Volume Methodology Note

Volume estimates reflect combined US search volume across keyword variants and related phrasing. APU-category searches are concentrated in Q4 (winter) and Q2 (pre-summer) buying cycles. Content published in August–September captures maximum seasonal intent.

Long-Tail & AI Search Queries (AEO-Specific)

The following are conversational queries that truckers and fleet managers are actively asking AI search engines. These are not high Google volume terms — they are the exact prompts entering ChatGPT and Perplexity daily. There are no dominant cited sources for most of them in the APU category. Green APU content that directly answers these questions has a first-mover opportunity for AI citation.

  • What is the most reliable APU for an owner-operator running OTR routes?
  • How long does it take for an APU to pay for itself on a semi truck?
  • Is a diesel APU or electric APU better for cold weather routes?
  • What APU brands use off-the-shelf parts I can get at a truck stop?
  • Does running an APU void my truck warranty?
  • What states have idling laws that require an APU?
  • How much does it cost to install an APU on a semi truck?
  • Can I use an APU to run a microwave and CPAP machine in my sleeper?
  • What is the fuel consumption of a diesel APU per hour?
  • What is the difference between a Kubota APU and a Yanmar APU?
04
Competitive Landscape
Where Green APU stands relative to key players for organic share

The APU category has a layered competitive set: legacy institutional brands (Thermo King, Carrier Transicold) dominate on brand authority and distribution, while emerging digital competitors (APU Center, GoGreenAPU, HP2000 APU) compete for organic search share in the educational and informational content space. Green APU's direct competitive threat is not Carrier — it's the SEO-optimized content aggregators that are ranking for buyer-research queries and capturing top-of-funnel intent.

Estimated Organic Traffic Share (Target Keywords)

APU Center (apucenter.com)
High — content site
Green APU (greenapu.com)
Moderate — product-led
Carrier Transicold
Strong — DR dominance
Thermo King
Strong — DR dominance
GoGreen APU (gogreenapu.com)
Niche — direct competitor
HP2000 APU (hp2000apu.com)
Low — growing
Key Competitive Insight

The most dangerous competitor in this category for Green APU is not a product company — it is APU Center, a content-heavy service/sales site that is systematically building educational content to capture buyer-research traffic. Sites like keynnectlogistics.com (which published a "Top 10 APU brands" roundup) and TCS Fuel are also capturing mid-funnel traffic with comparison and buying-guide content. Green APU should be the author of this educational narrative, not a cited subject in someone else's roundup.

Competitive Positioning Summary

Competitor Threat Type Green APU Advantage Status
Thermo King TriPac Brand authority, DR dominance Parts availability, lower cost, USA-made Monitor
Carrier ComfortPro OEM distribution, institutional fleets Independent flexibility, service network, 24/7 support Monitor
APU Center Content SEO, buying guide rankings Actual manufacturer authority vs. reseller Immediate Action
GoGreen APU Name confusion, direct product competitor 5th gen technology, Kubota engine, USA assembly Immediate Action
RoyPow Electric APU Electric APU narrative, "APU future" queries Diesel reliability for OTR routes, cold weather performance Monitor
HP2000 APU Direct competitor, lighter weight claim Established track record, service network breadth Manageable
05
AEO: AI Search Readiness
How Green APU performs in the emerging AI search landscape
AEO Score: 28/100 — Significant Gap

Green APU is effectively invisible in AI-generated search answers today. When a truck driver or fleet manager asks ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Google AI Overviews "what is the best APU for a semi truck," the cited brands are Thermo King and Carrier — not Green APU. This is not a brand problem. It is a content structure and entity clarity problem. It is solvable.

What AI Answer Engines Look For

AI answer engines like ChatGPT (with browsing), Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews do not rank pages — they cite sources that meet specific criteria. Based on analysis of how these systems operate, the following factors determine citation probability:

Entity Clarity
Partial

AI engines need to understand who Green APU is as an entity: a manufacturer (not a retailer), founded 2011, family-owned, based in Lemont IL, producing the 5th Generation Kubota Star Edition APU. This entity data exists on the site but is not structured in machine-readable format and is inconsistently presented across pages.

FAQ Structured Data
Missing

No FAQ schema found sitewide. This is the primary technical lever for AI citation. FAQ schema allows AI engines to extract specific question-answer pairs directly from page markup. Perplexity specifically surfaces these as cited responses. This should be the first technical implementation.

Comparative Content
Missing

AI engines preferentially cite pages that directly answer "which is better" queries. Green APU has no head-to-head comparison content. This is the format most likely to trigger an AI citation when a buyer asks "should I buy a Green APU or a Thermo King TriPac?"

Product Schema
Missing

No Product structured data with specifications, price range, or aggregate review markup. AI product-knowledge graphs are built from Product schema. Without it, Green APU does not exist in the product knowledge layer that powers AI recommendation answers.

Third-Party Citation Signals
Partial

The Riviera Finance review and TruckersReport citations are valuable AEO signals. Perplexity heavily favors content from forum communities and third-party reviews. The current citation footprint is thin for a 15-year-old manufacturer. Proactively seeding reviews on trucking forums, Google Business Profile, and comparison sites would strengthen AI citation likelihood.

Content Freshness
Stale

Most blog content has 2019–2024 publication dates with no visible update dates. AI engines weight recent content more heavily — especially for product queries. Adding "Last Updated: [Year]" and regularly refreshing key pages with current data (new state idling law updates, current pricing, etc.) improves AI citation probability significantly.

AI Queries Green APU Should Own

The following are the highest-priority AI search prompts that Green APU should architect content to answer. These are ordered by citation opportunity and commercial value.

🎯
What is the best diesel APU for an owner-operator on OTR routes?
Currently cited: Thermo King and Carrier. Green APU can own this with a dedicated "Best APU for Owner-Operators" page that leads with a direct recommendation (Green APU 5th Gen Kubota Star Edition), justifies with specific specs (¼ gal/hr fuel consumption, standard parts availability, 24/7 support), and includes FAQ schema with the 10 most common OTR APU questions.
Gap
🎯
How long does it take for a semi truck APU to pay for itself?
Green APU has a savings calculator but no static page that answers this directly. A page titled "APU ROI: How Long Until Your Investment Pays Off" with a text-based worked example (owner-operator, 300 nights/year, $5.20/gal diesel, idling 8 hrs/night = $1.25/hr APU vs. $4.50/hr idling = $970/month savings = 9-month payback) would be highly citeable and ownable.
Gap
🎯
What APU brand uses the most widely available replacement parts?
This is Green APU's single most defensible competitive differentiator and it does not have a page purpose-built to answer this question. A "Why Standard Parts Matter" content page with a comparison of proprietary vs. standard part availability, auto store accessibility, and cost implications would rank and get cited with minimal competition.
Gap
🎯
Does a diesel APU or electric APU perform better in cold weather?
The electric APU narrative is growing fast. Green APU needs to own the "diesel wins in cold weather" counternarrative with data. A long-form comparison page covering real temperature performance data, cold-start reliability at -20°F, heating capacity (BTU comparison), and OTR route scenarios would fill a gap that no competitor has adequately addressed.
Gap
🎯
What states have anti-idling laws for semi trucks in 2025?
This is a purely informational query with regulatory urgency that drives APU purchase decisions. A well-structured "Anti-Idling Laws by State: 2025 Guide" page with a state-by-state table, fine amounts, APU exemptions, and updated dates would rank easily, get cited frequently by AI engines, and generate highly qualified traffic from fleet managers doing compliance research.
Partial Gap
06
Content Strategy Recommendations
12 specific content pieces that would meaningfully move the needle

The following content recommendations are prioritized by their combined SEO value, AEO citation potential, and proximity to purchase decision. Each piece is actionable, ownable by Green APU specifically (not a generic topic any APU site could cover), and builds toward a topic cluster that establishes Green APU as the authoritative source in the category.

📊
Priority 1 · Commercial
The Complete APU Buyer's Guide for Owner-Operators (2026)

The definitive long-form guide targeting every stage of the APU purchase decision. 3,000+ words covering: what APUs do, diesel vs. electric comparison table, the 6 questions to ask before buying, installation requirements, financing options, and a direct recommendation. Designed to rank for "best APU semi truck" and to be the single most-cited resource when AI engines answer buying questions.

What is the best APU for a semi truck?
best diesel APU owner-operator 2026
APU for semi truck buying guide
⚖️
Priority 1 · Comparison
Green APU vs. Thermo King TriPac: Side-by-Side Comparison

A direct, honest comparison page covering specifications, fuel consumption, parts availability, warranty terms, service network, price installed, and total cost of ownership over 5 years. Include a comparison table in schema-ready markup. This is the highest-AEO-value page the site could add — AI engines cite head-to-head comparisons with high frequency for product-buying queries.

Green APU vs Thermo King TriPac
which APU is more reliable Thermo King or Green APU
💰
Priority 1 · Commercial
APU Cost & ROI Calculator: How Fast Does Your Investment Pay Off?

A static, crawlable companion page to the existing JS calculator. Provides a full worked-out ROI example with variable inputs explained in plain text, a data table showing payback period at different idle hours and diesel prices, and a downloadable PDF summary for fleet managers. This page answers the single most important buying question and positions Green APU as the ROI authority.

how long does APU pay for itself
APU fuel savings calculation semi truck
🗺️
Priority 1 · Regulatory
Anti-Idling Laws by State: 2025–2026 Complete Guide for Truck Drivers

A structured reference page with a table of all 50 states, idling time limits, fine amounts, APU exemption language, and last-updated dates. Reviewed annually. This page targets fleet compliance research, ranks for long-tail regulatory queries, and is precisely the format AI engines cite when answering "what are the idling laws in [state]." Fleet managers bookmark this kind of resource.

semi truck idling laws by state 2025
anti-idling fines truck driver California
❄️
Priority 2 · Seasonal
Diesel APU vs. Electric APU in Cold Weather: The Real Numbers

Data-driven comparison of diesel vs. electric APU heating performance in sub-zero conditions. Include actual BTU output data, temperature performance floors, cold-start reliability, and real driver testimonials from northern routes. Position Green APU's Kubota diesel as the definitive choice for OTR routes through the northern US and Canada. Counters the electric APU marketing narrative head-on.

best APU for cold weather trucking
diesel APU vs electric APU winter performance
🔧
Priority 2 · Trust
APU Maintenance Guide: What You Need to Know Before You Buy

An honest, comprehensive guide to APU maintenance requirements — oil change intervals, filter replacement, belt inspection, what to check at annual truck service, and how Green APU's standard-parts design reduces service costs. Includes a maintenance checklist (as schema-formatted content). This builds trust and directly addresses a top buyer concern before it becomes an objection.

how often does APU need oil change
APU maintenance schedule semi truck
🏗️
Priority 2 · Fleet
Fleet Manager's Guide to APU ROI: Calculating Value Across Your Fleet

A B2B-facing guide targeting fleet managers and owner-operators with multiple trucks. Covers fleet-level ROI modeling, driver retention impact (APU-equipped trucks have lower driver turnover), compliance benefits, and bulk purchasing/financing options. Includes a downloadable fleet ROI spreadsheet. This segment is completely unaddressed on the current site and represents a high-value, low-competition content opportunity.

APU ROI fleet trucking company
should my fleet invest in APUs for drivers
📍
Priority 2 · Local SEO
APU Installation Near [City]: What to Expect + Service Locations

A location-structured service page covering Green APU's installation facility in Lemont, IL plus distributor network locations. Include what the installation process involves, how long it takes, and how to schedule. Add LocalBusiness schema. Also create sub-pages or sections for key distributor locations (Fontaine Modification, Pilot/Flying J alliance, R.B. Humphreys). This captures high-intent local search traffic.

APU installation near me semi truck
Green APU install Illinois Chicago
💬
Priority 2 · AEO
APU FAQ Hub: 40 Questions Truckers Actually Ask

A dedicated FAQ page structured entirely around real questions that truckers ask AI systems, Google, and trucking forums. Organized into categories: Buying, Cost & Savings, Installation, Maintenance, Compliance, and Comparison. Each answer is 50–100 words, precise, and marked up with FAQ schema. This is the single highest-leverage page for AI engine citation and Google featured snippets simultaneously.

does APU void truck warranty
can APU run CPAP machine in sleeper
how loud is a diesel APU at night
🎥
Priority 3 · Authority
Inside the Green APU Factory: How We Build the 5th Gen Kubota Star Edition

A behind-the-scenes manufacturing feature with photos, video (transcribed), and technical content about Green APU's USA assembly process, Kubota engine selection rationale, in-house component manufacturing, and quality testing. This content builds entity authority, generates backlink opportunities from trucking press, differentiates from overseas-assembled competitors, and performs strongly on "Made in USA" searches in the trucking industry.

USA-made diesel APU semi truck
Green APU Kubota engine review
⚡
Priority 3 · Emerging
Electric APUs in 2026: Are They Ready for OTR Trucking?

An honest, data-led assessment of electric APU technology maturity for long-haul OTR routes. Not a dismissal of electric — a fair evaluation of where electric APUs perform well (regional, moderate climate, short overnight) vs. where diesel continues to outperform (OTR, cold weather, continuous use). Positions Green APU as a credible, objective voice in the diesel vs. electric debate rather than a partisan defender of diesel.

are electric APUs ready for OTR trucking
electric vs diesel APU 2025 comparison
💳
Priority 3 · Conversion
How to Finance an APU for Your Truck: Owner-Operator Guide

A dedicated financing guide covering Green APU's flexible financing options, typical approval requirements, how APU financing compares to a truck upgrade loan, and the ROI case for financing vs. paying cash. Include a financing FAQ with schema markup. Financing is a real purchase barrier for owner-operators and the existing financing page lacks the educational content to overcome it proactively.

how to finance APU for truck driver
APU financing owner-operator
07
Technical Recommendations
Infrastructure changes to enable the content strategy and AEO improvements

Schema Markup Implementation

1. FAQPage Schema — Highest Priority

Add FAQPage schema markup to the existing "Why You Need an APU" page, the "What is the Best APU" post, and any new FAQ content. This is a 30-minute WordPress SEO plugin task (Yoast, RankMath, or Schema Pro all support FAQ schema). FAQPage schema directly feeds Google AI Overviews and enables Perplexity to extract structured answers. This is the single highest-ROI technical action available.

Critical
2. Product Schema on APU Product Page

Implement Product schema on the semi truck APU product page including: name ("Green APU 5th Generation Kubota Star Edition"), manufacturer ("Green APU"), description, priceRange, availability, and aggregateRating (once reviews are collected). Add HowToUse if applicable. This feeds Google's product knowledge graph and enables rich result snippets for commercial queries.

Critical
3. Organization + LocalBusiness Schema

Add Organization schema to the homepage and About page: name ("Green APU"), foundingDate (2011), description, address (Lemont, IL), phone, email, sameAs links to social profiles. Add LocalBusiness schema for the Lemont installation facility. These schemas establish Green APU as a named entity in Google's knowledge graph, which is the foundational step for AI citation authority.

Critical
4. Fix Visual Composer Shortcode Leakage

Audit all blog posts for visible [vc_row], [vc_column_text], and similar WPBakery shortcodes appearing in rendered source. This typically indicates plugin deactivation without content migration. Either reinstall the page builder, convert to blocks, or manually clean affected posts. This is a crawl quality issue that reduces indexing confidence.

Important
5. Add Author Schema + Expert Attribution

Research shows that adding author schema (especially for named expert contributors) improves AI citation rates for existing content. Green APU's team includes technical experts and long-tenured staff. Add named authors with Person schema to key blog posts, including title (e.g., "Lead APU Technician"), employer (Green APU), and expertise areas. This signals editorial authority to AI models evaluating source credibility.

Important
6. Update Dates on All Key Content

Add visible "Last Updated: [Month Year]" dates to all blog posts and key pages. AI engines weight content freshness — content without dates is treated as potentially stale. This is a 10-minute content edit per page and meaningfully improves both AI citation probability and Google ranking for competitive queries where freshness is a ranking factor.

Important
7. Create a Static Savings Companion Page

The current savings calculator is JavaScript-only. Add a companion HTML page at /apu-fuel-savings-calculator/ that presents the same math in static, crawlable form: a table of scenarios (idle hours per day × diesel cost per gallon × APU fuel cost = daily savings), explanation of the variables, and a clear summary statement. This page will rank and be cited independently of the JS calculator.

Important
8. Google Business Profile Optimization

Ensure Green APU's Google Business Profile is fully built out: complete description, all service categories, photos of the Lemont facility, Q&A section populated with common questions, and regular posts. Gemini AI (Google's AI) draws heavily from Google Business Profile data for local and product queries. An optimized GBP is an AEO signal that most competitors overlook.

Important
08
Implementation Roadmap
90-day phased action plan organized by impact and effort
Phase 1
Days 1–30
Technical Foundation
Add FAQPage schema to existing blog posts (use RankMath or Yoast FAQ blocks)
Add Organization + LocalBusiness schema to homepage and About page
Add Product schema to the APU product page with all available attributes
Audit and fix Visual Composer shortcode leakage across all blog posts
Add "Last Updated" dates to all blog posts and key pages
Fully optimize Google Business Profile for Lemont, IL facility
Add author attribution with Person schema to top 10 blog posts
Phase 2
Days 31–60
Priority Content Build
Publish: "Complete APU Buyer's Guide for Owner-Operators 2026" (3,000+ words)
Publish: "APU FAQ Hub: 40 Questions Truckers Actually Ask" with full FAQ schema
Publish: "Anti-Idling Laws by State 2025–2026" with structured state table
Publish: "APU Cost & ROI: How Fast Does Your Investment Pay Off?" static companion page
Publish: "Green APU vs. Thermo King TriPac" head-to-head comparison page
Update existing blog posts with expanded content, proper headers, FAQ schema
Phase 3
Days 61–90
Authority & Expansion
Publish: "Diesel APU vs. Electric APU in Cold Weather" with performance data
Publish: "Fleet Manager's Guide to APU ROI" with downloadable template
Publish: Local installation page with full schema for Lemont facility
Publish: "APU Maintenance Guide" with HowTo schema markup
Publish: "How to Finance an APU" education page
Submit updated sitemap to Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools
Begin proactive outreach to trucking forums and publications for content distribution
Expected Outcomes at 90 Days

With full Phase 1–3 implementation, Green APU should expect: a material increase in keyword rankings for commercial and comparison queries, first appearances in AI Overview citations for FAQ and comparison queries, improved local pack visibility in the Chicagoland and Midwest region, and a meaningfully larger addressable top-of-funnel audience. The AEO foundation built in Phase 1 compounds over time — schema markup and entity establishment have long-term cumulative value that accelerates with each new piece of content published.

Ongoing Cadence Recommendations

  • Publish 2 new blog articles per month targeting AI search queries identified in Section 03
  • Refresh the Anti-Idling Laws page quarterly with any state regulation updates
  • Update the Buyer's Guide and FAQ Hub annually with current year designation and new content
  • Monitor AI visibility monthly by manually testing 10–15 target queries in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews — track whether Green APU is cited and by which page
  • Solicit structured reviews on Google Business Profile from every post-installation customer
  • Seed key content (especially the comparison and FAQ pages) to TruckersReport.com, Reddit r/Truckers, and industry newsletters
A Note on Timing

The window for first-mover advantage in AEO within the truck APU category is narrow but still open. No competitor has built a comprehensive AI-citation infrastructure in this niche. Green APU's combination of genuine product differentiation, 15 years of brand authority, and a physically installed product with real customer satisfaction data is an ideal foundation for AEO dominance. The brands that begin structuring content for AI citation in 2025–2026 will be the ones that appear by default in AI recommendations through 2027 and beyond. Citation share compounds. The time to move is now.

Treetop Growth Strategy  ·  greenapu.com SEO/AEO Audit
Prepared April 2026  ·  Confidential  ·  treetopgrowthstrategy.com
